title: What to know about enlarged spleen before using medicinal herbs
intro: Several herbal teas effectively support spleen health. Consider drinking tea made from dandelion root, an herb known for its ability to stimulate the spleen and promote healthy digestion. Milk thistle tea is another excellent choice, as it contains silymarin, a powerful antioxidant that aids in cleansing and detoxifying the spleen. Another option is red clover tea, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the spleen. Lastly, burdock root tea is well-known for its blood-purifying qualities, making it a popular choice for those seeking to improve spleen function.
definition: An enlarged spleen, also known as splenomegaly, refers to an abnormal expansion of this organ, which plays a crucial role in the immune system and old red blood cell removal. The spleen filters blood, destroying worn-out red blood cells, and storing red and white blood cells and platelets. A swollen spleen can result from various conditions, such as infections, blood disorders, or cancer. Ignoring an enlarged spleen can lead to severe complications, such as a ruptured spleen, which causes internal bleeding and requires immediate medical attention. Therefore, recognizing the signs of splenomegaly and seeking timely medical intervention is essential to preserve overall health and well-being.
